title: Deep learning and AI foundations
summary: Understand neural-network building blocks and keep the emphasis on what the model is optimizing, not on memorizing buzzwords.

items.prompt: In simple terms, what does a hidden layer do in a neural network?

solution.summary: It learns intermediate representations that help transform raw inputs into features useful for the final prediction.

hints: Think dataset exposure, not model architecture.

choices.label: One full pass through the training data.
A single neuron in the output layer.

solution.summary: An epoch is one complete pass through the training dataset during optimization.

hints: Training needs a target to minimize.
items.prompt: Why does a model need a loss function during training?
acceptedAnswers: to measure how wrong predictions are

solution.summary: The loss function quantifies prediction error so the optimization process knows how to adjust the parameters.

items.prompt: Before choosing a deep learning approach, what questions should you ask about the problem and the data?

solution.summary: Deep learning should be chosen because the problem demands it, not because it sounds more advanced.
checklist: Do I have enough labeled data for this task?
Is a simpler baseline good enough?
Can I explain, validate, and monitor the system responsibly?
